content:
I'm in the same boat, so I understand the pain.

We all use Thunderbird. But many users have needs that vary, and so we try to offer different capabilities.

In version 150, which comes out in a about 7 weeks (sooner in beta), the possibility of alpha order will return - you will be able to change the order to alphabetic, and also be able to set the number of folders shown.
creator: wsmwk

content: The config editor is a button on the settings>general page near the bottom. Click and the editor opens. Enter enough of the entry for its search to locate it. Once you see it, you can toggle on the right to switch from false to true.
creator: davidsk

content: You don't give us much information to go on, but I will guess that you have accidentally put Thunderbird into Offline mode. Check the menu item <File | Offline> and see if the Work Offline option is checked.
creator: rednoise

content:
I don't recall a quota bar, but you can check the percentage quota used by right-clicking on a folder and choosing Properties. There's a Quota tab there. Not very convenient, but it's there.

I did find mention of a Quota Bar add-on from long ago that appears to have been abandoned by the author.
creator: rednoise

content: Lin, Thanks for that, very handy. I had no idea it was there, Alan.
creator: Alan37

content: By default your used quota will appear in the status bar when you've reached 75%. You can change that setting in about:config (Config Editor... at the bottom of Settings > General) Look for mail.quota.mainwindow_threshold, there are three tresholds you can set. Set mail.quota.mainwindow_threshold.show to 0 to make your quota always visible.
creator: dillenger
